prerequisites: A Workday tenant (your organization's Workday instance) that exposes the REST API. You must know your tenant name, which goes in the tenant_id field.

The host of your Workday instance, for example wd5-services1.myworkday.com or wd2-impl-services1.myworkday.com. This is the hostname portion of your Workday URL and goes in the host field. Workday REST endpoints follow the form https://{host}/ccx/api/v1/{tenant}.

An API Client registered in Workday for the integration. In Workday, run the 'Register API Client for Integrations' task to create a client with the Authorization Code Grant or Client Credentials grant and the 'Bearer' access token type, then select the functional scopes the connector needs to read.

A valid OAuth 2.0 access token (Bearer token) for the registered API client, supplied via the credentials field. Long-running integrations typically generate a non-expiring refresh token using the 'Manage Refresh Tokens for Integrations' task tied to an Integration System User, then exchange it for access tokens.

Permission in Workday to register API clients and manage refresh tokens, plus an account (such as an Integration System User) with the security policy access required for the domains you intend to extract.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (Bearer access token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to the Workday REST API using OAuth 2.0. Provide the access token through the credentials object (credentials.access_token); it is sent as an HTTP Authorization: Bearer header on each request. Access tokens are issued for an API Client registered in your Workday tenant, commonly by exchanging a non-expiring refresh token created with the 'Manage Refresh Tokens for Integrations' task.

setupSteps.title: Register an API Client in Workday
setupSteps.body: In your Workday tenant, run the 'Register API Client for Integrations' task. Give the client a name, choose the grant type (for example Authorization Code Grant), set the access token type to 'Bearer', and select the functional scopes the connector should be allowed to read. Workday generates a Client ID and Client Secret for this client.

setupSteps.title: Obtain an access token
setupSteps.body: Generate a refresh token for the client using the 'Manage Refresh Tokens for Integrations' task tied to the integration account (for example an Integration System User), optionally marking it non-expiring. Exchange the refresh token for an OAuth 2.0 access token. This token is what the connector uses.

setupSteps.title: Identify your host and tenant
setupSteps.body: Find your Workday instance host (the hostname in your Workday URL, for example wd5-services1.myworkday.com) and your tenant name. REST requests resolve to https://{host}/ccx/api/v1/{tenant}, so both values are required.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector fields
setupSteps.body: Set host to your Workday instance hostname and tenant_id to your tenant name. Put the OAuth 2.0 access token in the credentials field (credentials.access_token). Optionally set start_date to bound the earliest records to sync and num_workers to control extraction concurrency.

sources.title: UiPath Integration Service - Workday REST authentication (OAuth 2.0, Bearer access token, client id/secret, refresh token)
Knit - Workday API Integration Guide (Register API Client, host/tenant, base URL https://{host}/ccx/api/v1/{tenant}, refresh/access tokens)
